title: Stable Bubble Sort with Incorrect Swap

statement.markdown: Given an array of integers, implement a stable bubble sort algorithm that sorts the array in ascending order. The algorithm should work correctly for nearly sorted arrays and arrays with duplicate values.
statement.inputFormat: The input will be an array of unique integers. The array size will be between 1 and 10,000. The elements will be between -10,000 and 10,000.
statement.outputFormat: The output should be a sorted array in ascending order.
statement.notes: The bubble sort algorithm should work correctly for nearly sorted arrays and arrays with duplicate values.

examples.explanation: The output should be a sorted array in ascending order.

hints.text: Check the swap logic in the bubble sort algorithm.

editorial.title: Stable Bubble Sort Algorithm
editorial.intuition: The bubble sort algorithm works by repeatedly swapping the adjacent elements if they are in wrong order.
editorial.bruteForce: The bubble sort algorithm has a time complexity of O(n^2).
editorial.optimalSolution: The optimal solution is to use a stable bubble sort algorithm.
editorial.proofOfCorrectness: The bubble sort algorithm is correct because it repeatedly swaps the adjacent elements if they are in wrong order.
